description: Keeper hooks: (1) events-writer appends one per-pid NDJSON line per hook invocation (lock-free, no SQLite) for the keeperd ingester to fold into the jobs projection; (1b) rename is a fail-open UserPromptSubmit exact-command hook for Keeper-managed Claude sessions: explicit canonical slugs return native sessionTitle without transcript work, bare rename snapshots and revalidates bounded transcript context before managed metadata inference, and every failure emits only fixed feedback; it always exits 0; (2) branch-guard is a PreToolUse(Bash) hard-deny that blocks subagents (agent_id present) from creating/switching git branches and from mutating git stash (list/show/create stay allowed) via the deny JSON envelope (still exits 0), keeping worker subagents on the current branch; (2b) grant-guard is a PreToolUse(Bash|Write|Edit|MultiEdit|NotebookEdit) hard-deny keyed on the hook payload's SUBAGENT IDENTITY (agent_type naming one of the four confined escalation agents: merge-resolver/deconflicter/unblocker/repairer) that denies a confined subagent's MUTATING calls unless a daemon-published owner-private grant leaf (src/grant-leaf.ts) validates the WHOLE tuple — parent job, exact agent type, incident instance + fencing identities, unexpired, matching monotonic fencing token — and its writable root covers the target; the unblocker never writes source (diagnosis-only), and protected paths (.git/config, git hooks, credentials, harness hook/MCP config) stay denied even under a valid grant; the Bash surface reuses the CVE-hardened quote-aware lexer + role classifier (interpreter one-liners, heredocs, redirects, tee, command substitution, env-runner wrappers, off-list/compound commands denied for every role) and a write-capable command is cwd-bounded to the granted checkout; it ENFORCES fail-closed in jurisdiction and stays inert (fail-open, no output) for any other agent_type and for identity-less top-level calls, always exiting 0; (2c) wrong-tree-guard is a PreToolUse(Write|Edit|MultiEdit|Bash) hard-deny that stops a worktree-LANE worker (KEEPER_PLAN_WORKTREE env marker set to the lane path) from writing into a tracked repo working tree that is not its own lane — resolving each write target (direct-tool file_path, and best-effort Bash redirect/heredoc/tee/sed -i vectors) and denying via the deny JSON envelope when it lands in a foreign .git-bounded tree, while allowing plan-state (.keeper), temp/home/state-dir, and own-lane writes and denying an unconditional .git/config + credential denylist regardless of lane; it FAILS OPEN on every internal error (best-effort audit, not a security boundary), stays inert for an unmarked session, and always exits 0; (2d) wrapped-guard is a PreToolUse(Write|Edit|MultiEdit|NotebookEdit|Bash) hard-deny that stops a wrapped-cell work:worker subagent (KEEPER_WRAPPED_CELL env marker non-empty AND agent_id/agent_type present) from editing source natively — a dumb-courier wrapper delegates all implementation to its provider leg via keeper agent run and owns only the keeper close-out, so this is a single-state total edit-denial: Edit/MultiEdit/NotebookEdit are denied outright, Write handoffs are descriptor-created exclusively at fresh inert .json/.txt/.md leaves in owner-private system-temp directories before the host Write is denied, and Bash is a POSITIVE allowlist (reject the whole shell-operator/expansion/redirect/heredoc/substitution surface and re-entrant wrappers up front, then allow only launch-bound non-Claude provider legs, task-bound commit-work/plan done, bounded reads/session state/baseline, and helper-disabled read-only git; repository tests/scripts and raw index/ref git stay denied) that denies every in-tree write vector as off-list; it FAILS CLOSED (deny) for a marked subagent on any unclearable command/payload and on parse ambiguity, stays inert (fail-open) for an unmarked human/native session, and always exits 0; (3) sidecar-writer is a fail-open PostToolUse(Write|Edit|MultiEdit|Bash) hook that owns the ~/docs metadata sidecar AND git state — create-or-merge a doc's .yaml on Write, upsert the gist-url on `gh gist create` (NEVER touching the .md), and commit every doc write/edit/delete to the ~/docs repo (pathspec-scoped, mechanical message, fail-open so a commit failure never blocks; still exits 0); (4) docs-pusher is a fail-open Stop hook that pushes ~/docs to its remote once per turn when local is ahead of @{u} (local ahead-count, no fetch), serialized by a .git/ lockfile and best-effort: a non-fast-forward/auth/network failure is logged to .git/keeper-push.log and skipped (never rebase, never --force) and it ALWAYS exits 0 — a Stop hook exiting non-zero would block Claude from stopping; (5) context-hint is a fail-open SessionStart hook that emits one additionalContext line pointing a session at the repo root's CONTEXT.md glossary when it is present and non-empty (absent/empty/unreadable/non-git cwd → emits nothing), a dep-free node-only fs walk that ALWAYS exits 0
